Message:
Add Part 3: testing guide with verified instructions

- Verified all existing buy.test.ts tests pass (4/4)
- Documented exact prerequisite steps (pnpm install + pnpm build --filter vercel...)
- Explained existing test patterns: client.scenario mocks, useUser(), toOutput(), stdin.write()
- Provided full example test skeleton for --visa flow grounded in real test infrastructure
- Documented VERCEL_VISA_TOKEN env var for CI test credential injection
- Fixed minor inaccuracy: output.success() not literal checkmark
- Added section on what NOT to test (no real API calls, no real payments)

Author: Dominik Ferber
Message:
[vercel flags] store hashed keys (#15027)

Patches the experimental embedding of flag definitions.

The changed functionality is enabled only enabled when the
`VERCEL_EXPERIMENTAL_EMBED_FLAG_DEFINITIONS` environment variable is
set. The existing `@vercel/flags-core` library is already
forwards-compatible with the new format.

See
https://vercel.com/docs/flags/vercel-flags/sdks/core#embedded-definitions

<!-- VADE_RISK_START -->
> [!NOTE]
> Low Risk Change
>
> This PR adds SHA-256 hashing of SDK keys before embedding them in
generated flag definition modules, which is a security improvement that
prevents raw keys from being exposed in build output.
>
> - Adds SHA-256 hashing of SDK keys to avoid embedding raw keys in
output
> - New `vercel flags prepare` CLI subcommand for manual flag definition
preparation
> - Comprehensive test coverage for hashing and module generation
>
> <sup>Risk assessment for [commit
46358e9](https://github.com/vercel/vercel/commit/46358e977c8ca4dfede7380af1091dafc9a0509f).</sup>
<!-- VADE_RISK_END -->

Description: Identified a Private Key, which may compromise cryptographic security and sensitive data encryption.
Author: Cody Wong
Message:
[CICD-DX] Improve env pull with conditional quoting of environment values (#14402)

# Improve Environment Variable Formatting in `vercel env pull`

This PR enhances how environment variables are formatted when using `vercel env pull` to create `.env` files:

- Added smart quoting for environment variables:
    - Values with spaces or starting with `#` are now properly quoted
    - Double quotes within values are escaped when necessary
    - Simple values remain unquoted for better readability
- Extracted formatting logic into a dedicated `formatEnvValue()` utility function with comprehensive test coverage
- Fixed newline handling to properly escape `\n` and `\r` characters

This change ensures that environment variables are formatted in a way that's compatible with standard `.env` file parsers while maintaining readability.

fixes [FLOW-879: `vercel env pull` cannot properly handle value that contains `"`​](https://linear.app/vercel/issue/FLOW-879/vercel-env-pull-cannot-properly-handle-value-that-contains)

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Luis Meyer
Message:
[cli] add blob command (#13364)

This PR adds cli commands to interact with Vercel Blob.

These are the new API's:
`vc blob store add mystore` - creates a new blob store, asks to connect
store to project and suggests pulling the new env var
`vc blob store rm store_mystoreid` - delete a blob store

All of the following commands will try to read the
`BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN` from the next `.env.local` file:
`vc blob put file.txt` - uploads the file from the path to the blob
store
`vc blob ls` - list blobs in the store
`vc blob del path/ path2/` - delete blobs from the store
`vc blob copy fromUrl toPathname` - copies a file within the store

# SDK update
Based on:
- OpenAPI Doc
- Speakeasy CLI 1.431.0 (2.451.0)
https://github.com/speakeasy-api/speakeasy
## TYPESCRIPT CHANGELOG
## core: 3.18.3 - 2024-10-31
### :bug: Bug Fixes
- Prevent panic with invalid implicit discriminator mappings with
propertyName within allOf object *(commit by
[@bflad](https://github.com/bflad))*

## core: 3.18.2 - 2024-09-30
### :wrench: Chores
- switch to ast.Arguments in TS SDKs *(commit by
[@disintegrator](https://github.com/disintegrator))*

## core: 3.18.1 - 2024-09-27
### :bug: Bug Fixes
- update readme formatting *(commit by
[@adaam2](https://github.com/adaam2))*

## envVarSecurityUsage: 0.1.2 - 2024-11-04
### :bug: Bug Fixes
- speakeasy examples override default strings *(commit by
[@ryan-timothy-albert](https://github.com/ryan-timothy-albert))*

## tests: 0.7.0 - 2024-09-12
### :bee: New Features
- download streams and alternate file upload names now supported in
tests *(commit by
[@tristanspeakeasy](https://github.com/tristanspeakeasy))*

## sdkHooks: 0.2.0 - 2024-11-02
### :bee: New Features
- allow hooks to trigger retries *(commit by
[@disintegrator](https://github.com/disintegrator))*

## enums: 2.82.1 - 2024-06-14
### :bug: Bug Fixes
- add explicit types for exported enum schemas *(commit by
[@disintegrator](https://github.com/disintegrator))*

## unions: 2.85.8 - 2024-11-05
### :bug: Bug Fixes
- improved handling of complex allOf schemas that merge multiple types
*(commit by [@tristanspeakeasy](https://github.com/tristanspeakeasy))*

Author: Trek Glowacki
Message:
Restore datadog checking with logs not exit codes (#10527)

Restores DataDog flakey test detection. Briefly disabled because the old method of using exit codes would bail the entire workflow.

Taking the Turbo team's suggestion of looking at `runData.execution` values to avoid an extra loop. Now with unit tests!

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Trek Glowacki
Message:
Restore datadog checking with logs not exit codes (#10527)

Restores DataDog flakey test detection. Briefly disabled because the old method of using exit codes would bail the entire workflow.

Taking the Turbo team's suggestion of looking at `runData.execution` values to avoid an extra loop. Now with unit tests!

Author: chloetedder
Message:
[fs-detectors] Add monorepo managers (#8534)

### Related Issues

Adding in Nx and Rush as monorepo managers.
This will allow to help with starting zero config for both the above managers.
I have added in unit tests for both Nx and Rush.

### 📋 Checklist

#### Tests

- [ ] The code changed/added as part of this PR has been covered with tests
- [ ] All tests pass locally with `yarn test-unit`

#### Code Review

- [ ] This PR has a concise title and thorough description useful to a reviewer
- [ ] Issue from task tracker has a link to this PR

Author: Nathan Rajlich
Message:
[frameworks] Add "hydrogen" framework preset (#8073)

Adds "hydrogen" framework preset for Shopify Hydrogen framework to allow for zero-config usage of the `@vercel/hydrogen` Builder.

Description: Detected a Generic API Key, potentially exposing access to various services and sensitive operations.
Author: Nathan Rajlich
Message:
[hydrogen] Add `@vercel/hydrogen` Builder (#8071)

Adds a new `@vercel/hydrogen` Builder package so that Vercel can support Shopify Hydrogen projects with zero config. It outputs an Edge Function for the server-side render code and includes a catch-all route to invoke that function after a `handle: "filesystem"` to serve static files that were generated by the build command.

**Examples:**

 * [`hello-world-ts` template](https://hydrogen-hello-world-otm2vmw6w-tootallnate.vercel.app/)
 * [`demo-store-ts` template](https://hydrogen-demo-store-1gko2fst3-tootallnate.vercel.app/)
